name: Cold Email System
description: A proven cold outreach campaign to land clients and customers without paid ads or social media
outcome: 10–20 positive replies and 3–5 booked calls per month

automation.followUp: Move to break-up sequence after 5 unanswered follow-ups

automation.action: Flag as warm lead + move to booking flow

assets.title: Email 1: The Hook (Day 1)
assets.content:
Subject: [Specific to their situation — not generic]
Subject line options:
• "Quick question about [their company/content/niche]"
• "[Mutual connection] suggested I reach out"
• "Idea for [their company name]"
• "[Specific observation about their business]"

---
Hey [First Name],

[PERSONALIZED OPENER — 1 sentence, specific to them]
Examples:
• "I saw your post about [topic] last week — your take on [specific point] stuck with me."
• "I noticed [Company Name] recently [launched/expanded/announced X]."
• "[Mutual contact] mentioned you're working on [thing]."

[THE BRIDGE — connect your observation to the problem you solve]
"Most [their role/industry] I talk to are dealing with [SPECIFIC PROBLEM] right now."

[YOUR HOOK — one sentence on what you do]
"I help [their type of business] [achieve specific outcome] — typically in [timeframe]."

[PROOF — one line, specific]
"[Client type similar to them] recently went from [before] to [after] using our [approach]."

[SOFT CTA — low-friction ask]
"Would it make sense to have a quick [15/20]-minute call to see if I could do the same for you?"

[Your Name]
[Title] | [Company]
[Phone if appropriate]

P.S. [A personal note or add-on value — e.g., a relevant resource, an observation, a connection]

assets.title: Email 2: The Value Add (Day 4)
assets.content:
Subject: Re: [Same thread — keep the original subject]

Hey [First Name],

Following up on my note from a few days ago.

In the meantime, I put together [a quick tip / a resource / a short video] specifically for [their role or situation] that I thought might be useful regardless of whether we work together:

[LINK TO FREE RESOURCE or paste a short, genuinely useful tip]

[WHY IT'S RELEVANT TO THEM — 1 sentence]

Is [the original problem you mentioned] still something you're actively working on? Happy to dig into your specific situation on a quick call.

[Your Name]

assets.title: Email 3: The Case Study (Day 8)
assets.content:
Subject: How [Similar Company/Person] got [Specific Result]

Hey [First Name],

Thought you'd find this relevant:

[CLIENT TYPE similar to them] came to me [timeframe] ago struggling with [SPECIFIC PROBLEM they likely have].

Here's what we did:
• [Step 1 — your approach]
• [Step 2]
• [Step 3]

Result after [timeframe]: [SPECIFIC MEASURABLE OUTCOME]

The approach is repeatable — I've seen similar results with [X other clients / companies like yours].

I think we could do something similar for [THEIR COMPANY/THEM].

Worth a 15-minute call this week to explore it?

[Calendly/scheduling link]

[Your Name]

assets.title: Email 4: The Objection Handler (Day 13)
assets.content:
Subject: I know what you're probably thinking

Hey [First Name],

I've sent a few notes and haven't heard back — which usually means one of a few things:

→ You're slammed and this keeps falling off the radar (fair — I get it)
→ You're not sure if [my service/approach] actually works for [their specific situation]
→ The timing just isn't right

If it's the middle one — let me address it directly:

[SPECIFIC OBJECTION RESPONSE — address the most likely hesitation for their situation]
Example: "I know it can feel like cold outreach is impersonal or pushy. The system I use is built around genuine personalization and value-first — every message is relevant and researched, not blasted."

And if it's timing — no pressure at all. Just let me know when would be better and I'll follow up then instead.

Either way, I'll leave the door open.

[Your Name]

assets.title: Email 5: The Break-Up (Day 18)
assets.content:
Subject: Closing the loop

Hey [First Name],

I've reached out a few times and haven't heard back — so I'm going to stop following up after this note.

If the timing isn't right or you're going in a different direction, I completely understand.

But if [THE PROBLEM YOU SOLVE] ever becomes a priority, I'd love to help. You can reach me at [email] anytime.

Wishing you all the best with [something specific about their work/business].

[Your Name]

P.S. If you have 10 seconds — could you let me know which of these is closest to the truth?
A) Not the right time
B) Not the right fit
C) Went with someone else
D) Still thinking about it

No judgment — just helps me improve how I reach out to people like you.

assets.title: Subject Line Swipe File
assets.content:
COLD EMAIL SUBJECT LINE SWIPE FILE
(40+ tested subject lines — copy, adapt, and split test)

CURIOSITY LINES:
• "Quick question, [First Name]"
• "Idea for [Company Name]"
• "[First Name] — saw this and thought of you"
• "Random thought about your [content/strategy/business]"
• "This might sound weird..."

PERSONALIZED LINES:
• "Re: your post about [topic]"
• "[Mutual connection] suggested I reach out"
• "Congrats on [recent achievement]"
• "Question about [something specific from their LinkedIn/website]"
• "[Company Name] + [Your Company Name]"

PROBLEM-AWARE LINES:
• "Struggling with [specific pain]?"
• "Most [their role] I talk to deal with this"
• "Is [specific challenge] on your radar?"
• "The [their industry] problem nobody talks about"

RESULT-FORWARD LINES:
• "How [Similar Client] got [Result] in [Timeframe]"
• "[X]% improvement in [metric] — here's how"
• "From [before state] to [after state]"
• "What I'd do differently if I were in your position"

CONVERSATIONAL LINES:
• "Worth a 15-minute chat?"
• "Can I share something with you?"
• "[First Name], honest question"
• "This probably isn't the right fit, but..."

RULES FOR SUBJECT LINES:
• Under 50 characters — many email clients cut off longer lines
• No ALL CAPS, excessive punctuation, or spam trigger words (free, guaranteed, buy now)
• A/B test: send 2 versions to different segments
• Curiosity > description — make them open to find out

assets.title: Reply Handling Scripts
assets.content:
REPLY HANDLING SCRIPTS
(Word-for-word responses to every scenario)

---
SCENARIO 1: Positive reply — they're interested
Their reply: "Sure, let's chat" / "Tell me more" / "What does this look like?"

Your response:
"Great, [First Name] — really glad this landed at the right time.

Here's a link to grab a time that works for you: [CALENDLY LINK]

The call is [15/30] minutes. I'll come prepared with [specific preparation — e.g., a quick audit of your current approach / 3 ideas specific to your situation].

Looking forward to it.
[Your Name]"

---
SCENARIO 2: Not now — come back later
Their reply: "Reach out in Q2" / "Try me in 3 months"

Your response:
"Completely understand — I'll circle back in [specific month].

I've set a reminder. In the meantime, I'll keep an eye on what you're building — looks like exciting things ahead.

[Your Name]"
[Then: actually set a calendar reminder]

---
SCENARIO 3: Wrong person — redirect
Their reply: "You should talk to [Name] — they handle this"

Your response:
"Thanks for the redirect, [First Name] — really appreciate it.

Do you mind if I mention your name when I reach out to [Name]? And should I email or connect on LinkedIn?

[Your Name]"

---
SCENARIO 4: Not interested
Their reply: "No thanks" / "We're all set" / "Not a fit"

Your response:
"Completely understand — thanks for the quick reply, [First Name]. I appreciate you taking the time.

Best of luck with [something specific]. If anything changes down the road, you know where to find me.

[Your Name]"
[Do NOT follow up again unless they reach out first]
